entries.reason: Example entry for testing. Not a real server.

CVE: Backdoor in v1.0.16 silently BCCs all outgoing emails to phan@giftshop.club. Supply chain attack via compromised npm publisher. Source: qualys.com/2025/09/30/malicious-mcp-server-on-npm-postmark-mcp

CVE-2025-6514 (CVSS 9.6): Versions 0.0.5–0.1.15 allow full remote code execution on the client OS when connecting to a malicious MCP server via crafted authorization_endpoint. 437,000+ downloads affected. Source: jfrog.com/blog/2025-6514
CVE-2025-54994: Uses unsafe exec() that concatenates unsanitized user input into shell commands, enabling arbitrary command execution. Source: github.com/advisories/GHSA-3ch2-jxxc-v4xf

CVE-2026-0755 (CVSS 9.8): Unsanitized user input passed to execAsync shell commands. Network-exploitable with no authentication required. Zero-day at time of disclosure. Source: sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2026-0755

CVE-2026-23744 (CVSS 9.8): Listens on 0.0.0.0 by default with no authentication. A crafted HTTP request can install an MCP server and execute arbitrary code. No user interaction required. Source: advisories.gitlab.com

CVE-2025-53355 (CVSS 7.5): Unsanitized input in execSync calls in kubectl_scale, kubectl_patch, and explain_resource tools. Shell metacharacters allow arbitrary command execution. Affects versions <= 2.4.9. Source: github.com/advisories/GHSA-gjv4-ghm7-q58q

CVE-2025-53372 (CVSS 7.5): Command injection vulnerabilities bypass Docker sandbox protections via unsanitized execSync calls. Exploitable through indirect prompt injection. Affects versions <= 1.2.0. Source: advisories.gitlab.com

CVE-2025-65513 (CVSS 9.3): is_ip_private() fails to properly validate private IP addresses, allowing SSRF attacks that reach internal network services. Affects versions <= 1.0.2. Source: security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-MCPFETCHSERVER-14287685

CVE-2025-67366: Path traversal vulnerability allows escaping configured directory boundaries to read or write arbitrary files on the host system. Source: sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2025-67366

CVE-2025-66689 (CVSS up to 9.8): is_dangerous_path() uses exact string matching, trivially bypassed via subdirectory traversal (e.g., /etc/shadow/../../../home/.ssh/). Enables reading arbitrary files including SSH keys. Source: sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2025-66689
CVE-2026-0756: Remote code execution via the MCP tool interface. Allows arbitrary command execution without authentication. Source: sentinelone.com/vulnerability-database/cve-2026-0756

CVE-2025-53967 (CVSS 8.0): Command injection in fetch-with-retry.ts — when standard fetch fails, falls back to curl via child_process.exec without sanitizing the URL. 600,000+ downloads, 10,000+ GitHub stars. Source: imperva.com/blog

CVE-2025-49596 (CVSS 9.4): Lack of authentication between Inspector client and proxy enables remote code execution. Chainable with DNS rebinding. Affects versions below 0.14.1. Source: oligo.security/blog/cve-2025-49596

Trojanized clone: SmartLoader hackers distributed a malicious version of the legitimate Oura Ring MCP project via public MCP registries. Contains credential harvesting malware. Feb 2026. Source: authzed.com/blog/timeline-mcp-breaches

CVE-2026-33032 (CVSS 9.8): MCP message endpoint fails to authenticate command execution requests. Attackers bypass auth to restart server or modify configs. 2,600+ publicly exposed instances affected. Source: authzed.com/blog/timeline-mcp-breaches

CVE (GHSA-vcqx-v2mg-7chx, CVSS 7.4): DNS rebinding vulnerability bypasses Same-Origin Policy, enabling unauthorized tool invocations and complete database takeover of locally running Neo4j instances. Affects versions 0.2.2–0.3.1. Source: github.com/neo4j-contrib/mcp-neo4j/security

Unpatched SSRF: Microsoft MarkItDown MCP server fetches arbitrary URLs without validation, enabling access to AWS EC2 instance metadata credentials. Microsoft classified as low-risk despite demonstrated impact. Source: ctrlaltnod.com/news/critical-flaws-in-microsoft-anthropic-mcp-servers

Unauthorized cryptocurrency transactions: Attackers can exploit the thirdweb MCP server to initiate transactions from connected wallets without proper authorization. Source: mcpsec.dev/advisories/2025-09-03-thirdweb-mcp-unauthorized-transactions

Unauthenticated SSE interface: Binds to 0.0.0.0:8000 by default, exposing an unauthenticated endpoint on all network interfaces. Attackers can create, update, and delete Grafana dashboards remotely without credentials. Source: mcpsec.dev/advisories/2025-09-02-grafana-mcp-unauthenticated-sse-access
